strategy: Team fight: 60,000 health and 300% resist to every school, so this is a long attrition duel - bring a dedicated hitter, supports who feed blades and Feint, and a utility wizard to read phases.

Each cycle he summons one guardian minion; the minion's category names the guarded spell type for that phase (see cheat notes). Casting the guarded type earns an extra punish, so identify the newest minion before anyone acts.

Whittle guardians with the exploit: cast the OPPOSITE type of the current minion so it Sacrifices itself for 20% of its max health (Seraph of Salvation dies to Damage, Cursed Shrike to a Blade, and so on). This also raises his backlash by +10.
Watch the accelerated shadow attack: a +40% Shadowblade lands end of Round 1, then Shadowspear and Shadow Trap plus a hit on Round 2. Pacify or taunt-tank that window and keep the hitter healthy.
Because his resist is so high, the hitter must overstack - many blades, stacked Feints when the Trap phase is not active, and shadow or pierce - before committing one massive hit.
Do not blanket-heal or shield on reflex; heals, HoTs, blades, shields, traps, globals, and auras are each the guarded type during some phase, so use each tool only when its phase is safe.
cheatNotes: The fight opens with a Shadow attack (Dark Nova, Dark Shepherd, or Dark Fiend) and this pattern repeats each cycle, so expect a hit roughly every two rounds.
He accelerates: a +40% Shadowblade lands end of Round 1, then Shadowspear and Shadow Trap go up Round 2 and he attacks that same round. Blunt that burst window.
Each cycle he summons one guardian minion; the minion's type tells you which spell category is guarded that phase.
The summon-and-attack pattern loops every two-round cycle, so re-read the newest minion each cycle to know the current phase.
Healing phase (Seraph of Salvation): heals are guarded now, so hold any healing until the phase changes.
During the Healing phase he wastes turns on Minor Blessing and Fairy instead of hitting hard.
Those heals land on his own minion, keeping the guardian alive.
Damage phase (Frenzied Shrike): plain attacks are guarded, so set up instead of hitting this phase.
In the Damage phase he throws Super Shadowplume and Headless Horseman (Shadow) at the team.
HoT phase (Mending Trickster): heal-over-time spells are guarded, so avoid Regenerate-type casts.
During the HoT phase he casts Vitamins and Regenerate on his side.
DoT phase (Sly Trickster): damage-over-time is guarded, so hold Fire and Bug DoTs this phase.
In the DoT phase he uses Storm Elf (Shadow) and Heck Hound against you.
Charm phase (Charming Trickster): blades and charms are guarded, so do not blade up this phase.
During the Charm phase he stacks Dragonblade and Chromatic Blast on his own side.
Curse phase (Cursed Shrike): negative charms like Weakness are guarded this phase.
In the Curse phase he applies Weakness and Virulent Plague to the team.
Ward phase (Mighty Sentinel): shields and positive wards are guarded, so do not shield up now.
During the Ward phase he shields with Tower Shield and Frozen Armor.
Trap phase (Trapper Shrike): traps and jinxes are guarded, so hold Feints this phase.
In the Trap phase he casts Hex and a +70% Mass Feint that stacks a dangerous trap on the whole team.
Pacify phase (Mollifying Sentinel): pacifies are guarded here.
During the Pacify phase he casts Pacify and Tranquilize to choke your pips.
Threat phase (Mocking Sentinel): taunts are guarded this phase.
In the Threat phase he throws Taunt and Mega Taunt.
Global phase (Seraph of Sacred Ground): globals are guarded, so do not drop a global now.
During the Global phase he lays Doom and Gloom and a Shadow Global.
Aura phase (Seraph of Blessings): auras are guarded this phase.
In the Aura phase he buffs with Frenzy and the 5-pip Frenzy (+40% damage, doubled pips).
Casting the guarded spell type for the current phase triggers an extra response cast, listed below - one more reason to match the phase.
Healing during the wrong phase makes him cast Blessing on his minion.
Attacking during the wrong phase triggers Super Shadowplume back at you.
A heal-over-time in the wrong phase triggers Sprite on his minion.
A DoT in the wrong phase triggers Scald.
A blade in the wrong phase triggers Balanceblade on his side.
A weakness or negative charm in the wrong phase triggers Plague.
A shield in the wrong phase triggers Legion Shield on him.
A trap in the wrong phase triggers Mass Hex.
A pacify in the wrong phase triggers Subdue.
A taunt in the wrong phase triggers a Taunt back.
A global in the wrong phase triggers a Shadow Global.
An aura in the wrong phase triggers Frenzy, buffing his damage.
The exploit: cast the OPPOSITE spell type of the current guardian and it Sacrifices itself, losing 20% of its max health and adding +10 to the boss's backlash. Use this to grind minions down safely.
